NYSE STOCK REPORT

SAN DIEGO, Thursday, May 07 2026 - Ahead of this morning's market opening, Sempra has shown some weakness in recent days. Sempra (NYSE:SRE), the NYSE's 4th largest Utility company by market cap, fell 2.2% during its previous trading session to close at $91.57.

Sempra (NYSE:SRE) is an American energy infrastructure company specializing in the development and operation of energy networks. The company is engaged in the construction and management of electricity grids and natural gas pipelines, offering reliable energy delivery services through its extensive network. Sempra's strategic investments in renewable energy projects and innovative technology solutions highlight its commitment to sustainability. With a robust pipeline of projects focused on clean energy transition, Sempra leverages its large-scale ecosystem and technological leadership. Key competitive advantages include its expansive supply chain and ability to innovate in the energy sector.

SECTION 1 RECENT NEWS

Recent News 1 of 2, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 258 words

Tue May 5, 2026: Sempra Strengthens Board with Jennifer M. Kirk Appointment

Sempra has announced the appointment of Jennifer M. Kirk to its board of directors, effective 20th June 2024. This strategic move comes as the company aims to enhance its governance and oversight, particularly in financial disciplines and risk management.

Kirk, who currently serves as the global controller and chief accounting officer at Medtronic plc, brings over two decades of experience in the energy sector. Her extensive background in finance and capital market activities will be instrumental in guiding the company's future strategies. The announcement was made on 6th May 2026 at 18:23:07.706818, highlighting the company's commitment to board refreshment and diversity.

Jeffrey W. Martin, the chairman and CEO, emphasized the importance of diverse perspectives in the boardroom, stating that Kirk's accounting and energy expertise will greatly benefit the company as it strives to solidify its position as North America's premier energy infrastructure provider. Kirk will also serve on the audit committee and the safety, sustainability, and technology committee.

Before her tenure at Medtronic, Kirk spent over 20 years at Occidental Petroleum Corporation, where she held various senior roles including senior vice president of integration and value capture. Her leadership experience in a major energy company aligns well with Sempra's objectives of operational excellence and sustainable practices.

With Kirk's appointment, Sempra's board now consists of ten directors, three of whom have been elected in the last five years, indicating a proactive approach to governance. The company continues to be recognized for its commitment to sustainability and high-performance culture, ensuring that it remains a leader in the energy infrastructure space.

Recent News 2 of 2, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 346 words

Sat Apr 18, 2026: Sempra Secures Regulatory Approval for Oncor's Rate Review

Sempra has announced a significant regulatory milestone as the Public Utility Commission of Texas (PUCT) approved a final order in Oncor Electric Delivery Company LLC's comprehensive base rate review proceeding. This approval, dated 20th April 2026, secures an annual revenue requirement of approximately $6.97 billion for Oncor, in which Sempra holds an 80.25% interest, marking an increase of about $560 million, or 8.7%, over Oncor's adjusted annualized revenues as outlined in the rate application.

The approved order includes a revised regulatory capital structure ratio of 56.5% debt to 43.5% equity, an authorized return on equity of 9.75%, and an authorized cost of debt of 4.94%. These figures indicate a positive shift from Oncor's previous authorized capital structure, which was 57.5% debt to 42.5% equity, along with a return on equity of 9.70% and a cost of debt of 4.39%. This change is expected to bolster Oncor's financial health, benefiting both Sempra and its investors.

Additionally, the order establishes a self-insurance reserve with an annual accrual amount in rates of $200 million for storm costs and other self-insured losses, a notable increase from the previous $122 million. A five-year amortization period for applicable regulatory assets and liabilities has also been set, excluding rate case expenses and year-end 2024 deferred system resiliency plan costs.

Oncor anticipates that this regulatory approval will have a positive impact on its future earnings, cash flow, and credit metrics. The company is permitted to surcharge the difference between the new billing rates and its current rates for the period from January 1, 2026, until the new rates take effect, which will be 45 days from the date of the final order. Oncor plans to file for this surcharge shortly after the new billing rates become effective, with recovery expected throughout 2026.

This development is a significant step forward for Sempra and Oncor, enhancing their financial stability and positioning them for future growth in the energy sector. Investors may want to evaluate the implications of this approval on Sempra's profitability and stock valuations, as understanding the business dynamics is crucial before making investment decisions.

SECTION 5 CORPORATE PROFILE

5.1 Activities

Sempra, a leading energy infrastructure company listed on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE: SRE), is a Fortune 500 holding entity headquartered in San Diego, California. The company's core business revolves around the development, operation, and investment in energy infrastructure, including natural gas utilities, pipelines, storage facilities, and electric services across North America. Through subsidiaries like San Diego Gas & Electric and Southern California Gas Company, Sempra delivers reliable energy to millions of customers, emphasizing safety, sustainability, and operational efficiency. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy projects and liquefied natural gas (LNG) exports, driven by increasing demand for clean energy solutions and global market expansion. For instance, Sempra's investments in wind, solar, and battery storage, as well as its LNG facilities in Mexico and the U.S., have fueled significant growth, positioning the company as a key player in the energy transition. With a strong focus on innovation and strategic partnerships, Sempra continues to enhance its It is the NYSE's 4th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

Sempra (NYSE:SRE) is strategically positioned as a powerhouse in the energy sector, leveraging a diversified business model focused on regulated utility rate increase dynamics and robust investments in energy infrastructure. As a key player, Sempra's comprehensive base rate approval, specifically the Oncor base rate review approval, strengthens its influence in the Texas electric utility base rate landscape. The company's operations, encompassing electric transmission and distribution services and natural gas pipelines and storage, showcase its commitment to energy infrastructure growth. Significant grid modernization at Oncor, paired with reliable utility capital expenditures (capex), underscores Sempra's proactive approach in enhancing transmission and distribution investment. Additionally, renewable energy projects Sempra, along with battery storage projects Sempra, highlight its dedication to sustainable energy solutions. With competitive strengths in Sempra regulated utilities (SoCalGas SDG&E Oncor) and a focus on regulated rate base expansion, Sempra offers promising avenues for Sempra investor earnings growth amid regulatory approvals and utility revenue requirement approval.

NASDAQ END-OF-DAY STOCK REPORT

www.buysellsignals.com

PETALUMA, Monday, May 04 2026 - Enphase Energy Inc.'s (NASDAQ: ENPH $32.53) stock price is down 3.9% after a downgrade in the past week. It was downgraded to Neutral by the analyst at Susquehanna.

Compared with the NASDAQ-100 Index which fell 58.5 points (0.2%) in the day, the relative price change was -3.7%.

There were 3,464,814 shares worth $112.7 million traded today; the volume was 0.5 times the average daily trading volume of 7.2 million shares. Since December 8, Enphase Energy Inc. has had thirty-two analyst rating changes with seventeen being upgrades. Enphase Energy Inc. is up 4.1% since December 8. Out of the 32 analysts we track, seventeen believe the stock price will go higher. The stock price is currently at a premium of 8% of the lowest analyst target price and at a discount of 52% of the highest analyst target price.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

Enphase Energy Inc., a Nasdaq-listed company headquartered in Fremont, California, is a leading innovator in the renewable energy sector. The company specializes in designing, developing, and manufacturing advanced microinverter systems, solar energy storage solutions, and intelligent software platforms that optimize energy production and consumption. At its core, Enphase focuses on enhancing the efficiency and reliability of solar power systems by converting direct current (DC) to alternating current (AC) at the module level, reducing risks associated with traditional string inverters. The fastest-growing segments include its IQ-series microinverters and Encharge battery storage systems, which have seen rapid adoption in residential and commercial markets due to increasing demand for grid-independent energy solutions and supportive renewable policies. This growth is fueled by technological advancements, such as integrated app-based monitoring and seamless compatibility with emerging smart home ecosystems, positioning Enphase as a key driver in the global transition to sustainable energy. It is the NASDAQ's 9th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

Lumber, including hardwood lumber and softwood lumber, forms the core of UFP Industries, Inc., NASDAQ:UFPI. Specializing in engineered wood and engineered panels, their product lineup features diverse offerings such as laminated veneer lumber (LVL), glulam beams, I-joists, and oriented strand board (OSB). They also provide particleboard and hardboard wood panels for various applications. Enhancing outdoor spaces, their decking choices include composite decking and plastic lumber, complemented by treated lumber for durability. UFP Industries further expands its portfolio with siding, exterior cladding, trim and mouldings that are integral to millwork. Their cabinet components and furniture components are meticulously crafted, alongside custom wood fabrication for tailored solutions. Hardwood flooring and laminate countertops represent their commitment to diverse interior finishes. Additionally, UFP Industries excels in producing packaging materials, pallet components, and crating materials, playing an operations integration role by ensuring seamless supply and delivery. This positions them as a comprehensive leader in the wood industry.

Tue Apr 28, 2026: Enphase Energy Unveils Solid-State Transformer for AI Data Centers

FREMONT, Calif. — Enphase Energy, Inc. (NASDAQ: ENPH), a global leader in energy technology, has announced the development of its IQ® Solid-State Transformer (IQ®SST), specifically designed for the evolving needs of AI data centers. The announcement was made on 28th April 2026 at 04:02 PM EDT, marking a significant step forward as the industry shifts towards higher-density DC power architectures.

The IQ SST aims to replace traditional centralized power conversion with a distributed ‘supercluster' architecture. Each unit will consist of a 1.25 MW rack that incorporates 342 intelligent, semiconductor- and software-defined power modules, achieving an impressive 98.5% efficiency and 99.999% availability through integrated redundancy. This innovative design allows operations to continue seamlessly, even with only 90% of the power modules functioning.

One of the standout features of the IQ SST is its elimination of internal batteries, which reduces or potentially removes the requirement for rack-level battery sidecars and traditional uninterruptible power supply (UPS) systems. For data center operators, this translates into reduced infrastructure costs, less space occupied by electrical equipment, and an increase in available floor space for GPUs. Enphase Energy's CEO, Badri Kothandaraman, stated, “AI is changing how power must be delivered to compute infrastructure,” underscoring the urgent need for this advanced technology.

The IQ SST is tailored to meet the demands of AI workloads that are driving data center power requirements from hundreds of kilowatts to megawatt-scale densities. With the capability to convert medium-voltage AC directly into regulated DC power in a single conversion stage, the platform supports both 800 VDC and +/-400 VDC configurations, essential for modern AI standards. This approach is expected to provide sub-millisecond response times to fluctuating AI loads, a necessity in today's fast-paced computing environment.

Enphase Energy plans to manufacture the IQ SST using the same automated platform that has successfully produced approximately 87.8 million microinverters. By sourcing standard high-volume semiconductor components domestically, the company aims to offer an alternative to overseas, custom-built power systems. Looking ahead, Enphase anticipates an annual addressable opportunity in the U.S. for IQ SST in AI data centers to exceed 11 GW by 2031, with customer pilots commencing in 2027 and volume shipments scheduled for 2028.

Mon Apr 27, 2026: Enphase Energy Partners with Evergen to Enhance Australian Home Battery Systems

Enphase Energy has unveiled a strategic integration between its IQ Batteries and Evergen, a prominent distributed energy and virtual power plant (VPP) orchestration platform utilized by various major energy retailers across Australia and New Zealand. This collaboration is set to enhance how homeowners effectively manage and capitalize on their solar and battery systems. Notably, on 27th April 2026, Enphase, recognized globally for its microinverter-based solar and battery solutions, announced that it supports over 65 VPP programs across eight countries, reinforcing its commitment to empower households to engage actively in grid services, thereby boosting energy flexibility and system efficiency.

Evergen's sophisticated optimization and VPP platform enables energy retailers to provide Enphase system owners with smarter energy management options. Homeowners can now charge their batteries during low electricity pricing periods, export stored energy back to the grid during peak pricing times, and participate in grid-support programs that financially reward users for flexible energy consumption. The availability of these features is contingent upon factors such as the retailer, system configuration, and local state regulations. Eligible homeowners may also take advantage of various financial incentives, including state-level programs in regions like South Australia and New South Wales, retailer sign-up bonuses, and ongoing participation credits.

Moreover, Australia's federal Cheaper Home Batteries Program offers a potential reduction of up to 30% on the upfront cost of eligible battery systems, subject to specific terms and conditions. Evergen states that this integration enhances both choice and value for Australian households, enabling them to optimize their Enphase battery systems, engage in VPP programs, and create new income opportunities while contributing to a more stable and resilient electricity grid. The confluence of federal rebates, state incentives, and retailer-driven programs is fostering robust momentum for home battery system adoption.

Through effective collaborations with platforms like Evergen and additional integrations such as Amber Electric, Enphase Energy customers are equipped with enhanced tools to minimize costs and maximize the value derived from their energy systems. Enphase emphasized that Australia remains a global leader in home energy innovation and views this integration as a pivotal move toward empowering homeowners with greater control over their energy usage, ultimately improving the return on their solar and battery investments. By merging financial incentives, advanced optimization platforms, and cutting-edge technology, this initiative is expected to accelerate the adoption of battery systems and provide a more efficient and flexible energy experience for consumers.

As Enphase Energy continues to push the boundaries of renewable energy solutions, the integration with Evergen signifies a significant step forward in the evolution of energy management for Australian homeowners. With the promise of smarter energy utilization and increased financial rewards, this partnership is anticipated to reshape the energy landscape, making it not only more efficient but also more economically viable for consumers.

Tue Apr 7, 2026: Enphase Energy Converts $235 Million Tax Credits into Cash Boost

FREMONT, CA— In a move that highlights the evolving financial architecture of the renewable energy sector, Enphase Energy (NASDAQ: ENPH) has successfully converted its domestic manufacturing prowess into cold, hard cash. The microinverter giant announced this week that it has sold $235 million in advanced manufacturing production tax credits (45X credits) at 93% of their face value. The transaction, finalized at the end of the first quarter, provides a vital liquidity injection of approximately $218.6 million, reinforcing the company's balance sheet as it navigates a complex landscape of shifting trade policies and cooling residential demand.

The sale represents one of the most significant applications of the Inflation Reduction Act's (IRA) "transferability" provision to date by a solar component manufacturer. While the transaction bolsters Enphase's cash position, it arrives with a notable accounting catch: the 7% discount on the credit sale is expected to shave roughly 6.7 percentage points off the company's GAAP gross margins for the first quarter of 2026. Despite this temporary reporting headwind, the move is being hailed by analysts as a masterful navigation of federal incentives designed to decouple the American energy supply chain from overseas volatility.

The journey to this $235 million monetization event began in mid-2024, when Enphase Energy (NASDAQ: ENPH) aggressively pivoted its manufacturing strategy to meet the "Domestic Content Bonus Credit" requirements. By ramping up production lines in the United States—leveraging partnerships with global manufacturing firms—Enphase began accruing Section 45X credits for every microinverter produced on American soil. The Tax Credit Transfer Agreement, finalized on March 31, 2026, and disclosed in an April 6 SEC filing, marks the culmination of that multi-year industrial shift.

Key stakeholders, including institutional investors and tax-equity partners, had been closely watching Enphase's ability to "harvest" these credits. Unlike traditional tax credits that require complex "tax equity" structures involving banks and equity flips, the IRA's transferability clause allowed Enphase to sell these credits directly to a profitable third-party entity for cash. The 93-cents-on-the-dollar rate reflects a standard market discount, accounting for the time value of money and the purchaser's assumption of tax recapture risks.

As we move deeper into 2026, Enphase faces a strategic crossroads. The short-term goal is clearly liquidity and the maintenance of its "premium" brand status amid a rise in Third-Party Ownership (TPO) models. With consumers shifting from purchasing solar systems to leasing them, Enphase must ensure its hardware remains the preferred choice for massive fleet owners and leasing companies like Sunrun (NASDAQ: RUN). The long-term challenge will be the potential for "incentive fatigue" or political shifts that could threaten the longevity of the 45X credits.

Apr 16, 2026: China Considers Solar Manufacturing Equipment Export Curbs Targeting U.S. Market, Posing Supply Chain Risk to Installer-Dependent Business Models

Chinese authorities have initiated consultations with solar panel equipment providers regarding potential limits on exports of advanced manufacturing technology to the United States, according to five people with knowledge of the discussions. China accounts for an estimated 80% of global solar panel component production and is home to the world's top ten suppliers of solar cell manufacturing equipment. Discussions with one equipment manufacturer focused on possible restrictions covering high-end machinery required for producing higher-efficiency panels using HJT technology. No formal rule has been finalized, and the consultations have not progressed to the stage of soliciting official industry feedback.

The prospective measure would extend export controls into an additional technology sector where China holds a dominant position, building on earlier restrictions applied to rare earth exports. The deliberations are taking place ahead of a planned bilateral summit the following month. The United States remains structurally dependent on imports for upstream solar components, with domestic solar cell manufacturing capacity of approximately 3.2 gigawatts against roughly 60 gigawatts of module production capacity, highlighting a structural gap in domestic supply that these restrictions could make harder to close.

If adopted, restrictions on advanced HJT manufacturing equipment could constrain the ability of U.S. solar manufacturers to scale domestic cell production, putting upward pressure on panel costs and potentially dampening installation growth across the residential and commercial solar markets.

Impact on Enphase Energy (ENPH)

Enphase Energy faces indirect but meaningful operational risk tied to a potential slowdown in U.S. solar panel manufacturing capacity expansion. Enphase's core business, encompassing microinverters and energy management systems, depends on robust residential and commercial solar installation activity, which in turn requires consistent access to competitively priced solar panels.

If China's potential export curbs constrain U.S. manufacturers' ability to scale domestic solar cell production using advanced HJT equipment, panel supply constraints or cost increases could dampen installation growth, reducing demand for Enphase's microinverters and storage systems. The domestic solar cell manufacturing capacity gap, at approximately 3.2 gigawatts against roughly 60 gigawatts of module production capacity, underscores the structural vulnerability of U.S. solar supply chains to upstream equipment restrictions.

Enphase's ongoing expansion into battery storage and virtual power plant services provides some degree of business diversification, but solar installation volume remains the primary driver of its addressable market growth in the United States.

Apr 14, 2026: Bloom Energy's Oracle Expansion and CFO Appointment Broaden Competitive Landscape for Enphase Energy in Distributed Power Sector

Bloom Energy appointed Simon Edwards as Chief Financial Officer, effective April 13, 2026. The appointment coincides with the company's 2.8 gigawatt distributed power partnership with Oracle Corporation, one of the largest on-site fuel cell commitments in the sector's history. Edwards assumes financial leadership as Bloom scales operations and capital deployment to fulfill this landmark hyperscaler agreement. The transition signals organizational readiness to manage the demands of gigawatt-scale distributed fuel cell deployment across Oracle's AI data center infrastructure. Bloom's solid oxide fuel cell systems deliver continuous, grid-independent on-site power generation, reinforcing the broader case for distributed energy as a foundational infrastructure strategy for large-scale computing facilities.

The Oracle partnership's scale validates distributed on-site generation as a deliberate and primary energy approach for hyperscalers, elevating the profile of the distributed energy sector broadly.

Impact on Enphase Energy: ENPH

Following this news, Enphase Energy faces a broadened competitive context within the distributed energy sector as Bloom's Oracle partnership demonstrates that on-site distributed power generation at hyperscaler scale is becoming a foundational infrastructure strategy. While Enphase's core residential and small commercial solar-plus-storage business does not directly compete with Bloom in the hyperscale data center segment, both occupy the distributed energy narrative that institutional investors and commercial procurement officers evaluate.

As Bloom builds a stronger reference portfolio in commercial distributed power, the sector's competitive and capital landscape broadens in ways that affect how Enphase's commercial expansion ambitions are assessed.

value.Title: Clean Energy Technologies Vermont Renewable Gas Reaches Agreement with State Environmental Regulators, Advancing Lyndon Renewable Energy Project

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHJ-FR23-RTP8-342B-00000-00Clean Energy Technologies Vermont Renewable Gas Reaches Agreement with State Environmental Regulators, Advancing Lyndon Renewable Energy Project2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:41:47ZLexisNexisClean Energy Technologies Vermont Renewable Gas Reaches Agreement with State Environmental Regulators, Advancing Lyndon Renewable Energy Project

AMERICAN DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

07 May 2026 00:12 EDT

ORIGINAL ANNOUNCEMENT [Text]

IRVINE, CA, May 06, 2026 -- Clean Energy Technologies, Inc. (Nasdaq: CETY) (CETY or the Company), a clean energy technology and solutions provider focused on converting waste and heat into power and fuels, today announced execution by its affiliate, Vermont Renewable Gas (VRG), of a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) with the Vermont Agency of Natural Resources, marking a significant milestone in the development of VRGs proposed 2.2 MW renewable energy facility in Lyndon, Vermont. The MOU, filed with the Vermont Public Utility Commission, reflects a comprehensive agreement on environmental and regulatory matters between VRG and Vermonts primary environmental oversight agency and establishes a clear framework for project approval and ongoing compliance.

A Project Aligned with Vermonts Environmental StandardsVermont has long been a pioneer and leading force in environmental sustainability action, and initiatives for local production of clean energy Under the terms of the MOU, the Project will proceed subject to rigorous, enforceable conditions, including:

Full compliance with all air quality, water, and stormwater permitsOngoing regulatory oversight and inspection by state agenciesPeriodic testing of biochar for environmental safety, including heavy metals and emerging contaminants, in coordination with the Vermont Agency of Agriculture, Food and MarketsSustainable sourcing of wood-based feedstock under an approved forestry management plan The Agency of Natural Resources has concluded that, subject to satisfying these conditions, the Project will not result in an adverse environmental impact, nor increased greenhouse gas emissions.

Addressing Community Priorities: Transparency, Safety, and AccountabilityCETY recognizes and respects the concerns raised by community members during VRGs public engagement process, particularly related to emissions, site operations, and project transparency.In response, CETY is committed to:

Project transparency, including providing detailed information on comparable operating facilities, with project data made accessible to VRGs stakeholdersOperational accountability, through supporting VRGs commitment to continuous monitoring, reporting, and third-party verificationCommunity engagement, together with VRG, ensuring ongoing communication with local stakeholders throughout development and operations This agreement represents an important step forward in this clean energy initiative, and reinforces our commitment to operating with full transparency and accountability, said Kam Mahdi.

ORIGINAL ANNOUNCEMENT [Link]

urn:contentItem:6JHM-8873-SHNH-21BC-00000-00OCTOPUS ENERGY GROUP - SECTOR INSIGHTS INCLUDING PEERS2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:41:48ZLexisNexisSTOCKOCTOPUS ENERGY GROUP - SECTOR INSIGHTS INCLUDING PEERS

DASHBOARD: OCTOPUS ENERGY GROUP

PermID5073147691Websitehttps://octopus.energy/IndustryRenewable Energy Supply & GenerationAddress"UK House, 5th floor, 164-182 Oxford Street London W1D 1NN England, United Kingdom"

ACTIVITIES:

Octopus Energy Group, founded in 2015 by Greg Jackson, is a renewable energy provider headquartered in London, UK. Employing over 3,500 people, it generates $5 billion annually, serving 7 million+ customers across 18 countries. Its Kraken platform optimizes energy usage. In 2024, Octopus expanded U.S. operations. Its culture emphasizes sustainability, innovation, and customer focus, with training in clean tech and AI. Competing with EDF, Octopus stands out for its flexible tariffs and smart grid tech. Recent initiatives include EV charging networks and net-zero commitments, aligning with green energy trends. Valued at $9 billion in 2023, Octopus drives the global energy transition.

+ OCTOPUS ENERGY GROUP [3 Press Releases]

+ OCTOPUS ENERGY GENERATION TO INVEST $500 MILLION TO REMOVE POLLUTING CO₂ FROM THE ATMOSPHERE

Major US deal to help cut up to 50 million tonnes of CO₂ - equivalent to eliminating the annual greenhouse gas emissions of New York City

Carbon credits to be snapped up by Google, Meta and McKinsey

Pushes Octopus closer to its ambitious $2bn US investment target, with a significant proportion in California's clean tech scene

London / San Francisco, 30 April 2026 - Octopus Energy Generation, one of Europe's leading renewables investors, is ramping up efforts to slash CO₂ pollution at scale - inking a major US deal that will help remove up to 50 million tonnes of carbon dioxide from the air.

Octopus's fund management team is set to invest $500 million in afforestation and reforestation projects in the US developed by public benefit and climate technology company Living Carbon.

Source: Company Website

+ POWERED UP: OCTOPUS AND MUIRHOUSE SLASH ENERGY BILLS FOR SCOTTISH SOCIAL HOMES BY £200

Muirhouse becomes one of Scotland's first housing associations to roll out Octopus Energy's groundbreaking Tenant Power tariff

UK's first green tech energy tariff that rewards both social housing landlords and tenants

Slashes electricity bills for social housing tenants by around £200 a year

Helping green home upgrades deliver real returns for landlords

London/Edinburgh, 29th April 2026 - Octopus Energy, the UK's largest energy supplier, has partnered with Muirhouse Housing Association to bring its groundbreaking Tenant Power tariff to over 30 social homes in Scotland.

Source: Company Website

+ 1 MILLION CUSTOMERS FOR EVERY TENTACLE: OCTOPUS ENERGY SUPERCHARGES TO 8 MILLION CUSTOMERS IN THE UK

The UK's largest energy supplier announces hitting 8 million home energy customers, with 2500 new customers joining daily

Market leading support for customers now includes You Pay We Pay, OctoAssist fund and the extension of their electric blanket scheme for Winter 2026

The milestone coincides with a doubling in heat pump sales and a 0.75 rise in solar sales as households shift to trusting in home grown power

London, 24th April 2026 - Octopus Energy has surpassed 8 million customers in the UK, further cementing its position as the nation's largest energy supplier.

With 2500 customers switching to Octopus every day, the gap has widened since Octopus claimed the top spot in January 2025, the first time the market lead had changed hands since privatisation in 1986.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD. [3 Press Releases]

+ ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD.: GOOD NEWS AND BAD NEWS: OUR BREAKING THE LINK CAMPAIGN IS WORKING - BUT WE NEED TO GO FURTHER

Britain's energy market is broken. For years, we've been campaigning to "break the link" between the soaring cost of fossil gas and the price of the cheap, green electricity we generate right here in Britain.

Now, the message is finally hitting home in the corridors of Westminster.

There was a flurry of excitement in mid-April 2026 as the chancellor, Rachel Reeves, announced that the government was looking at ways to break the link between electricity and fossil gas prices.

Source: Company Website

+ ECOTALK REINTRODUCE BEAVERS TO EAST SUSSEX AFTER A 400-YEAR ABSENCE

We've got amazing news from Ecotalk this spring with the release of a family of (extremely cute!) beavers into Bowyers Wood, East Sussex.

Chris Packham, Ecotalk's Chief Ecologist, was there to help the beavers get settled in. He said: "I just can't remember when I was last so excited about meeting a rodent! Big, fantastic rodents! What a fantastic day for Bowyers. And massive thanks to all our Ecotalk customers for making this possible."

Source: Company Website

+ ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD. OUR DAY WITH THE YOUNG GREEN BRITON CHALLENGE

Four members of the Ecotricity team recently took the hot seats as the judges at Stroud High School for entries to the Young Green Briton Challenge: a national programme that transforms students' climate ideas into real-world action.

For the 'Eco Dragon's Den', our team drew on their real-world expertise in green energy and sustainability. They used this to guide and challenge students on the impact and feasibility of their projects, offering constructive feedback to help sharpen each idea.

Source: Company Website

value.Title: Vermont Renewable Gas Reaches Agreement with State Environmental Regulators, Advancing Lyndon Renewable Energy Project

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHC-N023-S006-H2CW-00000-00Vermont Renewable Gas Reaches Agreement with State Environmental Regulators, Advancing Lyndon Renewable Energy Project2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:41:48ZLexisNexisPRESS RELEASESVermont Renewable Gas Reaches Agreement with State Environmental Regulators, Advancing Lyndon Renewable Energy Project

IRVINE, CA, May 06, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Clean Energy Technologies, Inc. (Nasdaq: CETY) (“CETY” or the “Company”), a clean energy technology and solutions provider focused on converting waste and heat into power and fuels, today announced execution by its affiliate, Vermont Renewable Gas (VRG), of a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) with the Vermont Agency of Natural Resources, marking a significant milestone in the development of VRG’s proposed 2.2 MW renewable energy facility in Lyndon, Vermont. The MOU, filed with the Vermont Public Utility Commission, reflects a comprehensive agreement on environmental and regulatory matters between VRG and Vermont’s primary environmental oversight agency and establishes a clear framework for project approval and ongoing compliance.

A Project Aligned with Vermont’s Environmental StandardsVermont has long been a pioneer and leading force in environmental sustainability action, and initiatives for local production of clean energy Under the terms of the MOU, the Project will proceed subject to rigorous, enforceable conditions, including:

Full compliance with all air quality, water, and stormwater permits

Ongoing regulatory oversight and inspection by state agencies

Periodic testing of biochar for environmental safety, including heavy metals and emerging contaminants, in coordination with the Vermont Agency of Agriculture, Food and Markets

Sustainable sourcing of wood-based feedstock under an approved forestry management plan

The Agency of Natural Resources has concluded that, subject to satisfying these conditions, the Project will not result in an adverse environmental impact, nor increased greenhouse gas emissions.

Addressing Community Priorities: Transparency, Safety, and AccountabilityCETY recognizes and respects the concerns raised by community members during VRG’s public engagement process, particularly related to emissions, site operations, and project transparency.In response, CETY is committed to:

Project transparency, including providing detailed information on comparable operating facilities, with project data made accessible to VRG’s stakeholders

Operational accountability, through supporting VRG’s commitment to continuous monitoring, reporting, and third-party verification

Community engagement, together with VRG, ensuring ongoing communication with local stakeholders throughout development and operations

“This agreement represents an important step forward in this clean energy initiative, and reinforces our commitment to operating with full transparency and accountability,” said Kam Mahdi. “Both CETY and VRG are dedicated to providing the level of visibility and access that the community and decision-makers expect, on an ongoing basis throughout the life of the project.”

A Critical Step Toward Project ApprovalThe MOU is expected to be incorporated into the Project’s Certificate of Public Good (CPG) proceeding before the Public Utility Commission and serves as a foundational element supporting final regulatory approval.

About the ProjectThe VRG facility is designed to convert locally sourced biomass and agricultural residuals into renewable energy, supporting:

Local energy generation and distribution

Reduction of waste streams

Long-term sustainable resource utilization

The Project is being developed with a focus on environmental stewardship, operational safety, and long-term community benefit.

About Clean Energy Technologies, Inc. (CETY)

Headquartered in Irvine, California, Clean Energy Technologies, Inc. (CETY) is a rising leader in the zero-emission revolution by offering eco-friendly green energy solutions, clean energy fuels and alternative electric power for small and mid-sized projects in North America, Europe, and Asia. CETY also holds a minority ownership interest in, and is affiliated with Vermont renewable Gas LLC. We deliver power from heat and biomass with zero emission and low cost. The Company's principal products are Waste Heat Recovery Solutions using our patented Clean CycleTM generator to create electricity. Waste to Energy Solutions convert waste products created in manufacturing, agriculture, wastewater treatment plants and other industries to electricity and BioChar. Engineering, Consulting and Project Management Solutions provide expertise and experience in developing clean energy projects for municipal and industrial customers and Engineering, Procurement and Construction (EPC) companies.

CETY's common stock is currently traded on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the symbol “CETY.” For more information, visit www.cetyinc.com.

Follow CETY on our social media channels: Twitter | LinkedIn | Facebook

About Vermont Renewable GasVermont Renewable Gas, LLC is a developer of renewable energy infrastructure focused on converting underutilized organic resources into sustainable power solutions. The Company is committed to advancing projects that meet stringent environmental standards while supporting local economies and energy resilience.

This summary should be read in conjunction with the Company’s quarterly report on Form 10-Q for the quarterly period ended September 30, 2025 and other periodic filings made pursuant to Section 13 or 15(d) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, which contain, among other matters, risk factors and financial footnotes as well as a discussions of our business, operations and financial matters located on the website of the Securities and Exchange Commission at www.sec.gov.

value.Title: Apple Announces Rs 100 Crore Investment In India For Renewable Energy Projects

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHM-GV63-S1GG-7117-00000-00Apple Announces Rs 100 Crore Investment In India For Renewable Energy Projects2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:41:47ZLexisNexisRENEWABLE ENERGY NEWS, Utilities news, Tech latest, Apple news, Environment news, India news & INDIAN SUBCONTINENT NEWSApple Announces Rs 100 Crore Investment In India For Renewable Energy ProjectsTimes Now Digital

Apple has announced a fresh set of environmental initiatives in India, with the Cupertino-based tech giant focusing on renewable energy, plastic waste reduction and support for green startups. The company says these efforts are part of its broader plan to become completely carbon neutral across its entire business footprint by 2030. As part of the new push, the iPhone maker plans to invest Rs 100 crore to help expand renewable energy infrastructure in the country.


Apple Expands Renewable Energy Plans In India
The US-based tech giant is partnering with renewable energy developer CleanMax to build more than 150 megawatts of additional renewable energy capacity in India. According to Apple, the planned infrastructure could generate enough electricity to power nearly 1.5 lakh Indian households annually. This is not the first time Apple and CleanMax have worked together. Earlier, the two companies collaborated on rooftop solar projects for Apple offices and retail stores in India. Now, the partnership appears to be expanding on a much larger scale. Apple also hinted that the collaboration may grow further over the next few years as the company continues increasing its clean energy footprint in the country.
'At Apple, our commitment to the environment is also a driving force for innovation- across the company and around the world,' said Sarah Chandler, Apple's vice president of Environment and Supply Chain Innovation. 'We're proud to expand our efforts to invest in India's clean energy economy and protect the country's precious natural resources.'
Focus On Plastic Waste Reduction
Alongside renewable energy, Apple says it is stepping up efforts to tackle plastic pollution in India. The Cupertino tech giant is working with WWF-India to support projects related to recycling and waste management. These programmes are expected to improve how plastic waste is collected, sorted and recycled while also helping prevent waste from entering surrounding ecosystems. While the company has not revealed specific targets yet, the initiative signals a wider environmental push beyond just energy infrastructure.
Support For Green Startups
Apple is also backing Indian entrepreneurs working on sustainability-focused businesses. Through a partnership with Acumen, the company will provide catalytic grants to six startups involved in sectors such as regenerative agriculture, waste management and circular economy solutions. According to Apple, the initiative is designed to help these startups grow faster by offering mentorship, technical guidance and access to industry networks. 'The program provides mentorship, strategic guidance, technical assistance, and network access to help social entrepreneurs validate promising business models and put them on a path to scale,' the tech giant explained.

value.Title: Q&A: Meet Rep. Scott Peters , candidate for California 's 50th Congressional District
value.Overview: The San Diego Union-Tribune: Web Edition (California), LATEST HEADLINES, 2229words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHT-8F43-RSSP-H156-00000-00Q&A: Meet Rep. Scott Peters, candidate for California's 50th Congressional District2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:41:48ZLexisNexisLATEST HEADLINESQ&A: Meet Rep. Scott Peters, candidate for California's 50th Congressional DistrictLucas Robinson

Rep. Scott Peters, 67, a Democratic member of Congress, is running for re-election to the 50th Congressional District in San Diego and inland North County.

Peters, originally from Ohio, is serving his seventh term in Congress representing San Diegans.

The San Diego Union-Tribune emailed a series of questions to Peters and other candidates to help inform voters about their positions, priorities and plans if elected.

Peters said he did not use any AI tools in responding to the Union-Tribune's questions.

1) Why are you running, and what makes you the best candidate? (150 words max)

I've delivered for San Diego; I want to keep succeeding for the region I love. I've passed laws to make prescription drugs more affordable for seniors, to make homeless services more accessible, to help families pay down student loan debt, to build renewable energy projects that protect against climate change and lower utility bills and to encourage more affordable housing near transit.

I've paid special attention to serving veterans. I've passed laws to ensure all Purple Heart veterans can access their full GI Bill education benefits and to allow more veterans to access housing assistance if they become homeless.

I've helped secure billions of dollars in federal funds for San Diego for our community colleges, to build mass transit and a new airport terminal and to repair aging stormwater systems to prevent flooding. I've secured hundreds of millions to rebuild the aging wastewater treatment plant to protect San Diegans from cross-border sewage.

2) What are the top 3 issues facing this district? (150 words max)

1. The housing crisis is one of the biggest impediments to prosperity and quality of life in San Diego and California. I have introduced legislation to lower the cost to build housing and to allow faith organizations to develop affordable housing on their property. I've strongly opposed Trump's tariffs, which make lumber and drywall more expensive.

2. Cross-border pollution from the Tijuana River Valley is threatening public health, military readiness, border security and our economy. I secured $650 million to fix and expand the dilapidated South Bay International Wastewater Treatment Plant. Now we must ensure projects on both sides of the border are completed quickly and that maintenance keeps pace.

3. Unusual "big weather" natural disasters will only get more severe and frequent from climate change. We must create a more resilient San Diego so families and businesses aren't hurt by future storms, floods and wildfires.

3) What are the first 3 things you would do in your first/next term in Congress? (150 words max)

First, we must speed up planning and permitting for clean energy projects so we can lower electricity costs and treat climate change like the crisis it is. I will continue my bipartisan efforts to ensure outdated laws don't keep us from tackling today's challenges.

I will continue to secure annual funds to maintain our wastewater treatment plant once it's built, and I'll plan cleanup efforts for the damage already done from cross-border sewage pollution.

I'll push for a bipartisan commission to get our fiscal house in order and ensure that the wealthiest Americans pay their fair share. We cannot saddle our kids with insurmountable national debt, and we cannot allow Social Security and Medicare to go insolvent in the next decade, which is what happens automatically if we don't act now.

4) If your party wins control of Congress, what should be its first priority? If your party loses control of Congress, what should be its first priority? (100 words max)

The Trump administration and his Republican-controlled Congress have failed to make life more affordable for Americans. When Democrats win, we must advance legislation that lowers housing, gas, grocery and healthcare costs. We must conduct oversight of Trump's administration, which has dismantled federal agencies and programs. Trump has instituted a pay-to-play system for his allies and unilaterally started wars while disregarding Congress's authority as a coequal branch of government. We must exercise oversight and assess damage done by Republicans' reckless spending spree that's added trillions to our national debt debt that is theft from young people who will foot the bill.

5) President Trump has made cracking down on immigration a cornerstone of his administration, ordering widespread arrests and detentions of immigrants nationwide and directing military resources to a new military zone along the U.S.-Mexico border. What impact have these had on this district? What are your goals on immigrants, immigration and the border, and how would you pursue them if elected? What is your message to constituents who are immigrants? (150 words max)

In May 2025, ICE agents stormed a South Park restaurant, threw the owner against the wall, handcuffed every employee and deployed flash bangs on concerned neighbors outside all to arrest four undocumented kitchen workers. This doesn't make us safer. It terrorizes neighborhoods, hurts businesses and innocent Americans have been killed.

The administration's all-of-government immigration enforcement operation creates a labor crisis and drives up costs for everyone. I agree we must secure our border, but I also view our location on an international border as an opportunity, not a threat.

I introduced the Stop Excessive Force in Immigration Act to set a higher standard for use of force by federal agents. It strictly limits harmful equipment, requires agents to render medical aid, requires clear uniforms, sets higher training standards, mandates the use of always-on body cameras and prohibits masks. Federal agents should respect the same standards as local law enforcement.

6) It's been nearly two months since the United States began a war with Iran. Do you believe Americans are better or worse off for it? What should Congress's role be? Would you vote to let the president continue the war beyond the 60 days after which congressional authorization is required? (150 words max)

The malicious Iranian regime is our national enemy. But after months of President Trump's unauthorized war-of-choice, Americans are worse off and less safe.

Before, Iran posed three threats development of a nuclear weapon, ballistic missiles and support for terrorism. Now, with thousands of bombs dropped, our stockpiles depleted, billions of taxpayer dollars spent and Iranian and American lives lost, we still face the same three threats. And Iran also knows that it can control the Strait of Hormuz. We've set off a worldwide energy crisis, and at home the cost of gas is at its highest level in four years.

Before I consider supporting the war through a war powers resolution or any funding via budget action, the administration must present a clear strategy and endgame to Congress and the American people that complies with domestic and international law, makes our country safer and allows international commerce to recommence.

7) Recent jumps in the cost of fuel, food and other goods combined with federal cuts to safety-net programs, new limits on certain federal loans and more are squeezing residents already struggling with San Diego County's high costs of living. What relief would you seek to offer, and how? (150 words max)

We should immediately 1) end Trump's unauthorized tariffs that raise prices for everyday goods; 2) end the mass deportation of immigrant workers, which increases the price of labor economy wide; 3) reverse cuts to Medi-Cal, which force care into expensive emergency rooms and raise

insurance premiums for everyone; and 4) end the war in Iran, which has caused the highest gas prices in years.

As the second-ranking Democrat on the Energy Subcommittee and chair of the New Democrat Coalition's Environment, Climate & Clean Energy Working Group, I'm leading efforts to lower energy costs by cutting permitting red tape that hinders the abundant and clean energy we need to keep up with demand. Delays cost over $100 billion per year. Also, because 30% of San

Diegan power bills are for wildfire response, I'm leading the Fix Our Forest Act to require the federal government to carry its own forest management load.

8) Like much of California, this district has been affected by the effects of climate change. How should Congress mitigate those impacts, and what would you do to pursue such efforts? (100 words max)

San Diego faces severe droughts, increased fire intensity and rising sea levels.

My Fix Our Forests Act, which passed the House on a strong bipartisan vote, will modernize forest management and help prepare communities like ours that are adjacent to wildlife areas.

In the wake of the January 2024 flooding around Chollas Creek, I'm working with the City ofSan Diego for federal funding for some of the most important stormwater control projects.

Our DROUGHT Act will modernize water project funding, and the National Climate Adaptation and Resilience Strategy Act will boost our resilience to extreme weather.

9) Polling underscores that one chief concern of California voters in the 2026 midterm elections is the issue of threats to democracy. What do you believe are the greatest threats to American democracy? How would you, and how should Congress, protect against these? (100 words max)

Trump has bypassed Congress's constitutional role in the assessment of tariffs, budgeting and declaring war. Congress should resist, but Speaker Johnson is a sleeping security guard. Trump's disregard for free and fair elections is alarming, starting with the Jan. 6 riots to thwart the will of the voters and the ensuing pardons. Trump has seized control of ballots in localities, suggesting that he may interfere with future elections. And the GOP's SAVE Act that purports to confront election fraud (which is almost nonexistent) would disenfranchise students, married women, servicemembers and rural Americans with draconian identification requirements. I oppose it.

10) Do you support a ban or restriction on congressional lawmakers and their families buying or selling individual stocks? Why or why not? And what would you propose to

ensure lawmakers aren't using their positions to engage in insider trading? (100 words max)

Yes, I support a ban on Congress members trading individual stocks. Many years ago, my wife and I moved our investments from stocks to mutual funds, where investment decisions are out of our control. We strictly comply with all reporting and disclosure requirements to avoid even the appearance of a conflict of interest.

However, this ban must cover the executive branch, too. The level of self-dealing in the Trump administration is beyond anything we've ever seen. To restore and strengthen public trust, we must call out how President Trump is using his office to enrich himself, his family and his allies.

11) By design Congress is supposed to serve as a check on the executive branch through budgetary, legislative and oversight powers. Do you believe that Congress has fulfilled that obligation in recent years? Why or why not? Should Congress do more to hold the president accountable? Explain. (150 words max)

No, it has not fulfilled that obligation! The lack of responsibility displayed by Republicans in Congress recently is unacceptable. Congress is an independently elected, coequal branch of government. Yet Speaker Johnson and House Republicans look the other way when President Trump has imposed tariffs, changed the budget and declared war all powers assigned by the Constitution to Congress.

The unprecedented corruption and self-dealing in the White House and the President's family go entirely without oversight by partisans who once complained about Hunter Biden. There is no accountability for the weaponization of the Justice Department or the issuance of pardons to Trump's friends and contributors from a Republican party that once held law and order as a sacred value. And the Senate has been complicit in confirming Trump loyalists not experts to judgeships and cabinet posts. I can't remember a time when Congress has been so impotent in exerting its constitutional authority.

12) A recent San Diego Union-Tribune poll found voters overwhelmingly disapprove of the job Congress is doing, by higher margins than they disapprove of the job Trump is doing. Why do you think this is? How can Congress win back voters' hope and trust, and what would you personally do to achieve this? (150 words max)

Republican leadership has abandoned our role as an independent, coequal branch. Each Congress member was elected the same day as the president. Republicans seem more scared of the president and of losing elections, so they don't fight for policies they once valued, like a balanced budget or rule of law.

Americans are frustrated nothing gets built, even after laws passed to address climate change and build infrastructure. Years later, only a fraction of projects are built because of burdensome red tape and outdated processes. Our challenges have not gone away but our capacity to achieve great things has. That's why I've taken on the challenge of reducing processes and focusing on production.

I will never forget that I represent San Diego. I go to Congress not for celebrity or publicity, but to get things done. That's why my record of accomplishment will always be the reason I ask for your vote.

Rep. Scott Peters is running for re-election in California's 50th Congressional District. (Courtesy)n
